revision.text:
The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[tomb]] or [[burial mound]] ("[[kofun]]" in Japanese) located near [[Asuka]] [[village]], [[Nara prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 meter in height and width and about 2.4 meters long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the Black Tortoise of the North, the Azure Dragon of the East, the Red Bird of the South, and the White Tiger of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also a star chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[archaeoastronomy]].
Fragments of a [[lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as important [[National Cultural Assets]] of Japan and [[World Heritage]] listed treasures, their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers was constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[mold]] spores and [[microorganisms]].
==External links== *[http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan]
==See also== *[[History of Japan]] *[[Kofun period]] *[[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]]
[[Category:Japan]] [[ja:キトラ古墳]]

[[File:Kitora tomb.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[tumulus]] (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[Villages of Japan|village]] of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the Black Tortoise of the North, the Azure Dragon of the East, the Red Bird of the South, and the White Tiger of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also a star chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as important [[National Cultural Assets]] of Japan and [[World Heritage]] listed treasures, their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers was constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[mold]] spores and [[microorganisms]].
==External links== *[http://www.nabunken.go.jp/english/palace.html Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]
==See also== *[[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[History of Japan]] *[[Kofun period]] *[[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] *[[Fujinoki Tomb]]

[[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[tumulus]] (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[Villages of Japan|village]] of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the Black Divine Tortoise of the North, the Azure Dragon of the East, the Red Phoenix of the South, and the White Tiger of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also a astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia. This type of stone-chamber tomb with mural paintings was influenced by ancient Korean kingdoms, especially Goguryeo(B.C. 1 C ~ A.D. 668, one of the kingdoms of ancient Korea. The proof is the astronomical chart which is depicted the night sky of Pyeongyang, the ancient capital of Goguryeo. Fragments of a [[lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as important [[National Cultural Assets]] of Japan and [[World Heritage]] listed treasures, their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers was constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[mold]] spores and [[microorganisms]].
==External links== *[http://www.nabunken.go.jp/english/palace.html Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties] * N. Hayakawa, [http://www.nippon.com/en/currents/d00270/ From Tomb to Museum: Transferring and Reconstructing the Kitora Burial Mound Murals], nippon.com (2017.03.09)

In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[microorganisms]].

{{Short description|Ancient tomb in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[tumulus]] (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[Villages of Japan|village]] of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983. [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[en:Kitora Tomb]][[Category:Category]] A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the [[Azure Dragon]] of the East, the [[Vermilion Bird|Red Phoenix]] of the South, and the [[White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[microorganisms]].

{{Short description|Ancient tomb in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[tumulus]] (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[Villages of Japan|village]] of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983. [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[en:Kitora Tomb]][[category:kk]] A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the [[Azure Dragon]] of the East, the [[Vermilion Bird|Red Phoenix]] of the South, and the [[White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[microorganisms]].

{{Short description|Ancient tomb in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[tumulus]] (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[Villages of Japan|village]] of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983. [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[Category:qq 3]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Kitora Tomb]][[Category:kk]] A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the [[Azure Dragon]] of the East, the [[Vermilion Bird|Red Phoenix]] of the South, and the [[White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[microorganisms]].

A gilded [[bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.

Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[microorganisms]].

{{Short description|Ancient tomb in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[tumulus]] (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[Villages of Japan|village]] of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983. [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[en:Kitora Tomb]][[Category:11]] A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the [[Azure Dragon]] of the East, the [[Vermilion Bird|Red Phoenix]] of the South, and the [[White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[microorganisms]].
==See also== *[[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[History of Japan]] *[[Kofun period]] *[[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] *[[Fujinoki Tomb]]
==External links== *[http://www.nabunken.go.jp/english/palace.html Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties] * N. Hayakawa, [http://www.nippon.com/en/currents/d00270/ From Tomb to Museum: Transferring and Reconstructing the Kitora Kofun Murals], nippon.com (2017.03.09)
{{Authority control}} {{Coord|34.4512|N|135.8050|E|format=dms|source:wikidata|display=title}}

A gilded [[bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[microorganisms]].

{{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:imported Kofun]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Kitora Tomb]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Kitora Tomb]] [[en:Kitora Tomb]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitora Tomb]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitora Tomb]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

{{Short description|Ancient tomb in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[tumulus]] (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[Villages of Japan|village]] of [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the [[Azure Dragon]] of the East, the [[Vermilion Bird|Red Phoenix]] of the South, and the [[White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[microorganisms]].
==See also== *[[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[History of Japan]] *[[Kofun period]] *[[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] *[[Fujinoki Tomb]]
==External links== *[http://www.nabunken.go.jp/english/palace.html Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties] * N. Hayakawa, [http://www.nippon.com/en/currents/d00270/ From Tomb to Museum: Transferring and Reconstructing the Kitora Kofun Murals], nippon.com (2017.03.09)
{{Authority control}} {{Coord|34.4512|N|135.8050|E|format=dms|source:wikidata|display=title}}

The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[microorganisms]].

{{Short description|Ancient tomb in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[tumulus]] (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[Villages of Japan|village]] of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[:en:Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the [[:en:Azure Dragon|Azure Dragon]] of the East, the [[Vermilion Bird|Red Phoenix]] of the South, and the [[White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[:en:Bronze|Bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[:en:Aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[microorganisms]].
==See also== *[[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[:en:History of Japan|History of Japan]] *[[Kofun period]] *[[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] *[[Fujinoki Tomb]]
==External links== *[http://www.nabunken.go.jp/english/palace.html Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties] * N. Hayakawa, [http://www.nippon.com/en/currents/d00270/ From Tomb to Museum: Transferring and Reconstructing the Kitora Kofun Murals], nippon.com (2017.03.09)
{{Authority control}} {{Coord|34.4512|N|135.8050|E|format=dms|source:wikidata|display=title}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient tomb in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[:en:Villages of Japan|village]] of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[:en:Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the [[:en:Azure Dragon|Azure Dragon]] of the East, the [[:en:Vermilion Bird|Red Phoenix]] of the South, and the [[:en:White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[:en:Archaeoastronomy|Archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[:en:Bronze|Bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[:en:Aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[:en:World Heritage|World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[:en:microorganisms|microorganisms]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[:en:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[:en:History of Japan|History of Japan]] *[[Kofun period]] *[[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] *[[Fujinoki Tomb]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kitora Kofun]][[en:Kitora Tomb]][[fr:Kofun de Kitora]][[ja:キトラ古墳]][[ko:기토라 고분]][[ms:Makam Kitora]][[zh:龜虎古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|location={{ill|Asuka|qid=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.4512|N|135.805|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Abe no Miushi|qid=Q11657245}}; {{ill|Prince Takechi|qid=Q837590}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|qid=Q4811855}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q846393]] Wikidata name: Kitora Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q846393]] Wikidata name: Kitora Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient tomb in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[:en:Villages of Japan|village]] of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[:en:Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the [[:en:Azure Dragon|Azure Dragon]] of the East, the [[:en:Vermilion Bird|Red Phoenix]] of the South, and the [[:en:White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[:en:Archaeoastronomy|Archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[:en:Bronze|Bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[:en:Aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[:en:World Heritage|World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[:en:microorganisms|microorganisms]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[:en:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[:en:History of Japan|History of Japan]] *[[Kofun period]] *[[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] *[[Fujinoki Tomb]]

In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[:en:Bronze|Bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[:en:Aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[:en:World Heritage|World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[:en:microorganisms|microorganisms]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kitora Kofun]][[en:Kitora Tomb]][[fr:Kofun de Kitora]][[ja:キトラ古墳]][[ko:기토라 고분]][[ms:Makam Kitora]][[zh:龜虎古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.4512|N|135.805|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Abe no Miushi|WD=Q11657245}}; {{ill|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q846393]] Wikidata name: Kitora Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q846393]] Wikidata name: Kitora Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient tomb in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[:en:Villages of Japan|village]] of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[:en:Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the [[:en:Azure Dragon|Azure Dragon]] of the East, the [[:en:Vermilion Bird|Red Phoenix]] of the South, and the [[:en:White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[:en:Archaeoastronomy|Archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[:en:Bronze|Bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[:en:Aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[:en:World Heritage|World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[:en:microorganisms|microorganisms]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[:en:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[:en:History of Japan|History of Japan]] *[[Kofun period]] *[[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] *[[Fujinoki Tomb]]
==External links== *[http://www.nabunken.go.jp/english/palace.html Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties] * N. Hayakawa, [http://www.nippon.com/en/currents/d00270/ From Tomb to Museum: Transferring and Reconstructing the Kitora Kofun Murals], nippon.com (2017.03.09)

In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[:en:World Heritage|World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kitora Kofun]][[en:Kitora Tomb]][[fr:Kofun de Kitora]][[ja:キトラ古墳]][[ko:기토라 고분]][[ms:Makam Kitora]][[zh:龜虎古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.4512|N|135.805|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Abe no Miushi|WD=Q11657245}}; {{ill|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q846393]] Wikidata name: Kitora Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q846393]] Wikidata name: Kitora Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient tomb in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[:en:Villages of Japan|village]] of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[:en:Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the [[:en:Azure Dragon|Azure Dragon]] of the East, the [[:en:Vermilion Bird|Red Phoenix]] of the South, and the [[:en:White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[:en:Archaeoastronomy|Archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[:en:Bronze|Bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[:en:Aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[:en:World Heritage|World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[:en:microorganisms|microorganisms]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[:en:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[:en:History of Japan|History of Japan]] *[[Kofun period]] *[[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] *[[Fujinoki Tomb]]
==External links== *[http://www.nabunken.go.jp/english/palace.html Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties] * N. Hayakawa, [http://www.nippon.com/en/currents/d00270/ From Tomb to Museum: Transferring and Reconstructing the Kitora Kofun Murals], nippon.com (2017.03.09)

The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[:en:Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the [[:en:Azure Dragon|Azure Dragon]] of the East, the [[:en:Vermilion Bird|Red Phoenix]] of the South, and the [[:en:White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[:en:Archaeoastronomy|Archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[:en:Bronze|Bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[:en:Aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[:en:World Heritage|World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[:en:microorganisms|microorganisms]].

{{wikidata link|Q846393}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kitora Kofun]][[en:Kitora Tomb]][[fr:Kofun de Kitora]][[ja:キトラ古墳]][[ko:기토라 고분]][[ms:Makam Kitora]][[zh:龜虎古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kitora Kofun]][[en:Kitora Tomb]][[fr:Kofun de Kitora]][[ja:キトラ古墳]][[ko:기토라 고분]][[ms:Makam Kitora]][[zh:龜虎古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kitora Kofun]][[en:Kitora Tomb]][[fr:Kofun de Kitora]][[ja:キトラ古墳]][[ko:기토라 고분]][[ms:Makam Kitora]][[zh:龜虎古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.4512|N|135.805|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Abe no Miushi|WD=Q11657245}}; {{ill|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q846393]] Wikidata name: Kitora Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q846393]] Wikidata name: Kitora Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient tomb in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[:en:Villages of Japan|village]] of [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[:en:Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the [[:en:Azure Dragon|Azure Dragon]] of the East, the [[:en:Vermilion Bird|Red Phoenix]] of the South, and the [[:en:White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of [[:en:Archaeoastronomy|Archaeoastronomy]]. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a [[:en:Lacquer|Lacquer]]ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded [[:en:Bronze|Bronze]] fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of [[:en:Aristocracy|aristocratic]] background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] of Japan and [[:en:World Heritage|World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[:en:microorganisms|microorganisms]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[:en:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[:en:History of Japan|History of Japan]] *[[Kofun period]] *[[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] *[[Fujinoki Tomb]]

The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[:en:Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the {{ill|Azure Dragon|en|Azure Dragon|WD=Q515882|lt=Azure Dragon}} of the East, the {{ill|Vermilion Bird|en|Vermilion Bird|WD=Q1059392|lt=Red Phoenix}} of the South, and the [[:en:White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of {{ill|Archaeoastronomy|en|Archaeoastronomy|WD=Q463923|lt=Archaeoastronomy}}. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a {{ill|Lacquer|en|Lacquer|WD=Q11236878|lt=Lacquer}}ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded {{ill|Bronze|en|Bronze|WD=Q34095|lt=Bronze}} fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of {{ill|Aristocracy|en|Aristocracy|WD=Q123432|lt=aristocratic}} background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}} of Japan and [[:en:World Heritage|World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[:en:microorganisms|microorganisms]].

{{Short description|Ancient tomb in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus}} (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[:en:Villages of Japan|village]] of {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[:en:Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the {{ill|Azure Dragon|en|Azure Dragon|WD=Q515882|lt=Azure Dragon}} of the East, the {{ill|Vermilion Bird|en|Vermilion Bird|WD=Q1059392|lt=Red Phoenix}} of the South, and the [[:en:White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of {{ill|Archaeoastronomy|en|Archaeoastronomy|WD=Q463923|lt=Archaeoastronomy}}. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a {{ill|Lacquer|en|Lacquer|WD=Q11236878|lt=Lacquer}}ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded {{ill|Bronze|en|Bronze|WD=Q34095|lt=Bronze}} fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of {{ill|Aristocracy|en|Aristocracy|WD=Q123432|lt=aristocratic}} background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}} of Japan and [[:en:World Heritage|World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[:en:microorganisms|microorganisms]].
==See also== *{{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|WD=Q20982902|lt=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments}} *{{ill|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|WD=Q3245518|lt=en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)}} *{{ill|History of Japan|en|History of Japan|WD=Q130436|lt=History of Japan}} *[[Kofun period]] *[[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] *[[Fujinoki Tomb]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kitora Kofun]][[en:Kitora Tomb]][[fr:Kofun de Kitora]][[ja:キトラ古墳]][[ko:기토라 고분]][[ms:Makam Kitora]][[zh:龜虎古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kitora Kofun]][[en:Kitora Tomb]][[fr:Kofun de Kitora]][[ja:キトラ古墳]][[ko:기토라 고분]][[ms:Makam Kitora]][[zh:龜虎古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kitora Kofun]][[en:Kitora Tomb]][[fr:Kofun de Kitora]][[ja:キトラ古墳]][[ko:기토라 고분]][[ms:Makam Kitora]][[zh:龜虎古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.4512|N|135.805|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Abe no Miushi|WD=Q11657245|1=Abe no Miushi}}; {{ill|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590|1=Prince Takechi}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855|1=Asuka Historical National Government Park}}}}
{{Short description|Ancient tomb in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Kitora 201611a.jpg|250px|thumb|right|The Kitora Tomb]] [[File:Kitora Tomb2.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Rear view of building that houses the Kitora Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Kitora Tomb'''|キトラ古墳|''Kitora Kofun''}} is an ancient {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus|1=Tumulus}} (''[[kofun]]'' in Japanese) located in the [[:en:Villages of Japan|village]] of {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tomb is believed to have been constructed some time between the 7th and early 8th centuries, but was only discovered in 1983.
A small stone chamber, the Kitora Tomb is a little over 1 metre in height and width and about 2.4 metres long, just large enough to bury a single person. The four walls are aligned with the cardinal points of the compass, and respectively feature the [[:en:Black Tortoise|Black Divine Tortoise]] of the North, the {{ill|Azure Dragon|en|Azure Dragon|WD=Q515882|lt=Azure Dragon|1=Azure Dragon}} of the East, the {{ill|Vermilion Bird|en|Vermilion Bird|WD=Q1059392|lt=Red Phoenix|1=Vermilion Bird}} of the South, and the [[:en:White Tiger (China)|White Tiger]] of the West. On the ceiling of the chamber there is also an astronomical chart that has been the focus of much research and debate by scholars in the field of {{ill|Archaeoastronomy|en|Archaeoastronomy|WD=Q463923|lt=Archaeoastronomy|1=Archaeoastronomy}}. In addition, the 12 zodiac animals-headed figures with human body are painted on the wall, which may be one of the oldest remaining zodiac murals in East Asia.
Fragments of a {{ill|Lacquer|en|Lacquer|WD=Q11236878|lt=Lacquer|1=Lacquer}}ed wooden coffin, torn apart when the tomb was robbed, lay 5 cm thick on the chamber floor, mixed with grave goods and human bone. A gilded {{ill|Bronze|en|Bronze|WD=Q34095|lt=Bronze|1=Bronze}} fitting and sword decorations were discovered, both executed with superbly inlaid patterns. Based upon analysis of the bone fragments and items found in the tomb, it is believed the interred was a middle-aged or older male of {{ill|Aristocracy|en|Aristocracy|WD=Q123432|lt=aristocratic|1=Aristocracy}} background.
The paintings have suffered the ravages of time, and, as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} of Japan and [[:en:World Heritage|World Heritage]], their preservation has been accorded the highest priority. The entire tomb has been roofed over, and a series of adjoining antechambers were constructed to isolate the central chamber from temperature and humidity fluctuations, and prevent contamination by airborne [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]] spores and [[:en:microorganisms|microorganisms]].
==See also== *{{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|WD=Q20982902|lt=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|1=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments}} *{{ill|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|WD=Q3245518|lt=en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)}} *{{ill|History of Japan|en|History of Japan|WD=Q130436|lt=History of Japan|1=History of Japan}} *[[Kofun period]] *[[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] *[[Fujinoki Tomb]]
==External links== *[http://www.nabunken.go.jp/english/palace.html Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties] * N. Hayakawa, [http://www.nippon.com/en/currents/d00270/ From Tomb to Museum: Transferring and Reconstructing the Kitora Kofun Murals], nippon.com (2017.03.09)
